title: The Last Call
description: 11:47 PM. An anonymous call. A voice that claims to know something you'd hoped to bury. You have until midnight.
author: Terminal Stories

text: 11:47 PM. Your phone buzzes on the nightstand.
Unknown number.
You pick up, because detectives always pick up.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Detective Morel. You have 13 minutes before it all comes crashing down."

choices.action: Your voice is calm. Professional. It's a reflex.
choices.consequence: "Someone who knows what happened on November 14, 2019."

choices.action: You set the phone down.
choices.consequence: It rings again instantly. "Next time you hang up, the envelope goes to the press."

choices.text: Stay on the line and try to trace the call
choices.action: You grab your second phone and start a trace.
choices.consequence: "Don't waste your time with that. VPN on top of VPN. You have 12 minutes."

text: November 14, 2019.
The Leclerc case. A suspect dead in custody. Officially: cardiac arrest.
You know what really happened. You and Durand.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "You remember? Of course you do."

choices.text: "I don't know what you're talking about."
choices.action: A self-protective reflex. Old, worn out.
choices.consequence: "The hallway camera. Number 4. The one you thought was broken."

choices.action: You stay silent. You let it talk.
choices.consequence: "Smart. Here's what I want: go to 12 Rue de la Roquette. Alone. Now."

text: Camera number 4. Corridor B. You had checked every camera.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Well... Durand had checked. You trusted him, didn't you?"

text: The floor tilts slightly beneath your feet.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "12 Rue de la Roquette. Come alone. You have 11 minutes."

choices.action: You dial Durand's number.
choices.consequence: Voicemail. You leave a vague message. He'll call back, or he won't.

choices.action: You dress quickly. You take your weapon.
choices.consequence: Out on the street, the air is cold. Paris is quiet at this hour.

text: 12 Rue de la Roquette. A Haussmann-era building. A keypad on the door: 1419.
You go up. Third floor. The door is ajar.
Inside: a chair, a lamp, an envelope on a table.
And a voice in your earpiece, because your phone is still connected.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "The envelope. Open it."

choices.action: Your hands don't shake. That matters.
choices.consequence: Photographs. Camera number 4. You. Durand. And the truth, in high-resolution JPEG.

choices.action: You check every room before touching anything.
choices.consequence: The bedroom is empty. But in the bathroom: a man, bound, gagged. Conscious.

text: The photos are sharp. Damning.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "There's a USB drive in the envelope. The full video is on it. Backed up in several copies, safe."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "What I want isn't money. I want you to do something for me."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Meet Durand tomorrow. Tell him what you know. And record the conversation."

choices.action: You ask the question you shouldn't have needed to ask.
choices.consequence: "Because he's the one who left the camera running. Ask yourself why."

choices.text: "I won't do it. Publish whatever you want."
choices.action: You set the photos down. You stand.
choices.consequence: A silence. Then: "Interesting. I thought you had more to lose."

text: You remove the gag. The man is breathing fast. Fortyish, suit disheveled.
"They told me... that if I talked..." He swallows. "I'm the technician. From camera number 4."
In your earpiece:

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Ah. You found him. Good. That simplifies things."

choices.text: Free the technician and get him to talk
choices.action: You undo his bindings. He straightens up with difficulty.
choices.consequence: "It was Durand who paid me. To keep the camera 4 files. For five years."

choices.action: "What do you want me to do with him?"
choices.consequence: "That's yours to decide, Detective. You always knew how to decide."

text: If Durand left the camera running knowingly...
Six years as partners. Six years he could have exposed you. And he didn't.
Or he was waiting for the right moment.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "You have 6 minutes. The recording, or the envelope goes to the press."

choices.action: You dial his number. This time he answers.
choices.consequence: "Morel? It's midnight. What's going" His voice shifts. "You're at la Roquette?"

choices.action: "All right. I'll make the recording."
choices.consequence: "Wise choice. The envelope stays sealed... as long as you cooperate."

text: The technician looks at you with eyes that have seen too much.
"Durand wanted insurance. Against you. In case you turned on him first."
The twisted logic of it sinks in slowly.
In the earpiece:

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "You understand now. Two people protecting each other by holding each other hostage."

choices.action: "Give me his address." The technician hesitates. "I already know it."
choices.consequence: You step out into the night. The voice in your earpiece finally goes quiet.

choices.action: You dial the Internal Affairs line. Hands steady.
choices.consequence: "You're turning yourself in?" The Voice sounds... almost admiring.

text: Silence.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: Then: "You're bluffing. But it's brave."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Fine. New proposal."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "I don't want to destroy you. I want the truth exposed. Help me do it cleanly."

choices.action: You sit back down. You listen.
choices.consequence: "A statement. Complete. Against you and Durand. In exchange: no charges for you."

choices.action: "No."
choices.consequence: The line goes dead. The silence is total.

text.speaker: player
text.text: "How do you know I'm at la Roquette?"

text: A two-second pause. Too long.

text.speaker: partner
text.text: "I... I got a message. Anonymous. With your address."

text: He knew. He'd known since this night began.

choices.text: "You're the one who left the camera running."
choices.action: You say it out loud for the first time.
choices.consequence: A long silence. "Yes." Just that. One word that changes everything.

choices.action: You don't reveal what you already know.
choices.consequence: "On my way." He hangs up. You wait. The envelope is still on the table.

text: The technician watches you. He doesn't look dangerous.
But he knows. He's known for five years.
The Voice waits. The technician waits.
Everyone is waiting for you to decide.

choices.text: Free the technician and leave without doing anything else
choices.action: "Go. Get out. And forget this night."
choices.consequence: He leaves fast. Very fast. The Voice: "You'll let him talk, one day."

text: His voice is tired. Old.

text.speaker: partner
text.text: "I needed insurance. You know how it works. You can't afford to trust."

text.speaker: partner
text.text: "Not even the people you respect."

text: Outside, Paris keeps sleeping. In here, something is ending.

choices.text: "Meet me tomorrow. We'll settle this together."
choices.action: You give him one last chance.
choices.consequence: He agrees. He'll come, or he won't. You'll know tomorrow.

text: Durand arrives ten minutes later. He sees the photos on the table.
He says nothing for a long time.

text.speaker: partner
text.text: "So now you both know."

text: The Voice is still in your earpiece. Watching.

choices.action: Your voice is calm. Professional. Despite everything.
choices.consequence: "For the same reasons you would have, in my place."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "Tomorrow morning. The prosecutor's office. Tell them everything."

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "In exchange, I'll testify that you cooperated. And the envelope stays sealed."

text: You look at the photos. Your career. Ten years.

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "You have 2 minutes to decide."

choices.action: "All right."
choices.consequence: The line goes dead. You're alone in the empty apartment.

text: You find yourself at Durand's door at 1 AM.
He opens it before you knock. He was expecting you.
You talk until dawn. Everything is said. Nothing is resolved.
But sometimes, naming things is a beginning.
The Voice never calls again.
You'll never know who it was.
— ENDING: Between the Two of Us —
The next morning, you walk into the prosecutor's office.
Durand is already waiting there. He did the same on his end.
You look at each other without a word before the door opens.
What follows is long. Painful. Just.
You lose your badge. You keep something else, something you'd been without for a long time.
— ENDING: The Price of Truth —
The recording is made. Durand is arrested.
The Voice disappears. So does the envelope.
You keep your badge. You lose your partner.
And every night, you wonder whether you acted out of justice
or out of fear.
The difference between the two is smaller than people think.
— ENDING: The Informant —
Nothing happens.
A week passes. Then a month.
The envelope is never published.
Maybe the Voice never intended to.
You'll live with that uncertainty.
It may be lighter than the truth.
— ENDING: Uncertainty as Refuge —
He comes.
You sit across from each other, the photos between you.
You decide together. What you'll do. What you'll say.
Whether it's the right decision, you'll know in ten years.
Or maybe never.
— ENDING: A Choice Made Together —
You look at Durand. You see yourself.
Two people who did the same thing. For the same reasons.
The Voice in your earpiece:

text.speaker: voice
text.text: "There. Now you understand."

text: And the line goes dead forever.
What you make of this night is yours.
— ENDING: Facing the Mirror —
